name: Digital Functional Skills — Level 1
_notes: Section A is the auto-marked knowledge test. Questions follow the Pearson sample L1 paper style: contextual stems with one clear correct answer and three plausible distractors. Each question's `area` field maps to the official content statement (e.g. 1.3 = sponsored search results).

mcqs.context: Some internet search results are sponsored.
mcqs.stem: Where would you see the sponsored results in a list of search results when you use an internet search engine?
mcqs.answer: At the top

mcqs.stem: How can you usually recognise a sponsored search result?
options: It appears in a different language
It has a smiley face icon
mcqs.answer: It is labelled 'Ad' or 'Sponsored'

mcqs.stem: Why is the first result in a list of sponsored search results not always the best?
options: The business paid to appear there — it isn't ranked on quality alone
Search engines list the worst result first
It is in the wrong language
mcqs.answer: The business paid to appear there — it isn't ranked on quality alone

mcqs.stem: Which website is most likely to give reliable information about a medical condition?
mcqs.answer: The NHS website

mcqs.stem: Which is the BEST sign that information online is reliable?
options: Author and date are clearly shown
mcqs.answer: Author and date are clearly shown

mcqs.stem: Why might a company's own website be a less balanced source of information about its products?
options: It only shows the company in a positive light
mcqs.answer: It only shows the company in a positive light

mcqs.stem: Why does the publication date of a web article matter when researching a topic?
options: Older information may be out of date
Older articles are always more reliable
The date changes the website's colour
Recent articles cost more to read
mcqs.answer: Older information may be out of date

mcqs.stem: What does copyright on an online image mean?
options: You cannot use it without permission from the owner
It is free for anyone to use
It is for online use only
mcqs.answer: You cannot use it without permission from the owner

mcqs.stem: Which file name is most appropriate for a project meeting from January 2026?
mcqs.answer: 2026-01-Project-Meeting.docx

mcqs.stem: Which of these can you use to find a file when you only remember part of its name?
mcqs.answer: The file explorer search box

mcqs.stem: How can you find every PDF file on your computer at once?
options: Open each folder one by one
Print the contents of each folder
mcqs.answer: Search for *.pdf in file explorer

mcqs.stem: Why might you use a hierarchical folder structure for a project (folder → sub-folder → file)?
options: It keeps related files grouped and easier to find
mcqs.answer: It keeps related files grouped and easier to find

mcqs.stem: How can you start a document on a laptop and continue it on a phone?
options: Email it to yourself once a week
mcqs.answer: Save it to cloud storage

mcqs.stem: You share a cloud file as 'View only'. What can the recipient do?
options: Read it but not change it
mcqs.answer: Read it but not change it

mcqs.stem: Why is cloud version history useful?
options: It lets you restore an earlier version of a file
mcqs.answer: It lets you restore an earlier version of a file

mcqs.stem: Which of these is the largest size?
mcqs.answer: 1 gigabyte

mcqs.context: You have a smartphone with 5000 kilobytes of free space.
mcqs.stem: Which one of these file sizes do you have space to download?
mcqs.answer: 50000 bytes

mcqs.stem: Which is the LARGEST unit of digital storage in this list?
mcqs.answer: TB

mcqs.stem: Approximately how many megabytes (MB) are in 1 gigabyte (GB)?
What is a consequence of file compression?
mcqs.answer: Reduces data transfer time

mcqs.stem: Which file type usually contains other files compressed together?
mcqs.answer: .zip

mcqs.stem: Why might an online service refuse to attach a very large file to an email?
options: The file is over the service's attachment size limit
The file is the wrong colour
Files cannot be attached to emails
mcqs.answer: The file is over the service's attachment size limit

mcqs.stem: Which icon on an application is most likely to give you help?

mcqs.context: An application has crashed twice this morning showing the message 'Error 0x80070005'.
mcqs.stem: What is the most useful next step to find a solution?
options: Search online for the error code and the application name
Send a screenshot to a stranger
mcqs.answer: Search online for the error code and the application name

mcqs.stem: A printer says 'offline'. What is a sensible first check?
options: Check the cable / Wi-Fi between the printer and computer
Send an email to the manufacturer
mcqs.answer: Check the cable / Wi-Fi between the printer and computer

mcqs.stem: Why is restarting a device often a good first step when something is wrong?
options: It clears temporary problems in memory
It updates the operating system fully
mcqs.answer: It clears temporary problems in memory

mcqs.stem: When would it be sensible to reinstall an application?
options: When the app keeps crashing even after restarting
mcqs.answer: When the app keeps crashing even after restarting

mcqs.stem: Which spreadsheet formula adds the values in cells C4 to C11?
mcqs.answer: =SUM(C4:C11)

mcqs.stem: Which formula calculates the mean of the values in cells B2 to B10?
mcqs.answer: =AVERAGE(B2:B10)

mcqs.stem: Which formula returns the highest number in the range D2:D20?
mcqs.answer: =MAX(D2:D20)

mcqs.stem: Which formula returns the lowest number in the range D2:D20?
mcqs.answer: =MIN(D2:D20)

mcqs.stem: Which formula multiplies the value in cell B4 by the value in cell C4?
What is the cell reference for the cell in column C, row 7?
mcqs.answer: C7

mcqs.stem: Every spreadsheet formula must start with which character?
Which chart is most suitable for showing parts of a whole, like sales by sandwich type?
mcqs.answer: Pie chart

mcqs.stem: Which chart is best for showing how a value changes over time, e.g. monthly sales?
options: Stacked bar with no time axis
mcqs.answer: Line chart

mcqs.stem: Which chart is best for COMPARING quantities across categories?
mcqs.answer: Bar / column chart

mcqs.stem: Which of these is essential on most charts to make them clear?
options: A clear title and axis labels
mcqs.answer: A clear title and axis labels

mcqs.stem: What format should you apply to a column of prices in a spreadsheet?
mcqs.answer: Currency

mcqs.stem: Which format should you use for a column showing growth like 12% and 7%?
mcqs.answer: Percentage

mcqs.stem: How would you display the value 3.14159 as £3.14 in a spreadsheet?
options: Apply Currency format with 2 decimal places
Round the value down then retype
mcqs.answer: Apply Currency format with 2 decimal places

mcqs.stem: What does sorting a column A→Z do?
options: Arranges the data alphabetically from A to Z
mcqs.answer: Arranges the data alphabetically from A to Z

mcqs.stem: Sorting a column of prices ascending will arrange the prices in which order?
mcqs.answer: Lowest to highest

mcqs.stem: Why might you merge cells A1:H1 in a spreadsheet?
options: To create a single centred title across the table
mcqs.answer: To create a single centred title across the table

mcqs.stem: Which feature forces text to start at the top of the next page in a Word document?
mcqs.answer: Page break

mcqs.stem: Which page orientation is wider than it is tall?
mcqs.answer: Landscape

mcqs.stem: Why use headings and bullet points in a long document?
options: They make the document easier to scan and read
mcqs.answer: They make the document easier to scan and read

mcqs.stem: What does CROPPING an image do?
options: Cuts off parts of the image you don't want
Makes the image black and white
mcqs.answer: Cuts off parts of the image you don't want

mcqs.stem: When resizing a photo for a document, why is it usually important to keep the proportions?
options: To stop the image looking stretched or squashed
mcqs.answer: To stop the image looking stretched or squashed

mcqs.stem: What does changing the CONTRAST of an image do?
options: Adjusts the difference between light and dark areas
Adds new colours that weren't there
mcqs.answer: Adjusts the difference between light and dark areas

mcqs.stem: Which greeting is most appropriate in a work email to a manager you have not met?
mcqs.answer: Dear Mr/Ms Smith

mcqs.stem: Which is the most appropriate closing for a formal work email?
mcqs.answer: Kind regards

mcqs.stem: What is the purpose of an automated email signature?
options: Automatically adds your name and contact details to every email
mcqs.answer: Automatically adds your name and contact details to every email

mcqs.stem: Which header keeps recipients hidden from each other?
mcqs.answer: Bcc

mcqs.stem: Which message is most appropriate for a customer service team?
options: Hi, my order #1234 hasn't arrived. Could you check the status please? Thanks.
mcqs.answer: Hi, my order #1234 hasn't arrived. Could you check the status please? Thanks.

mcqs.stem: Which is the most appropriate tone for an instant message to a close friend?
mcqs.answer: Casual and friendly

mcqs.stem: What can you do to limit your digital footprint?
mcqs.answer: Use private/incognito browsing

mcqs.stem: Why might you reject third-party cookies on a website?
options: They are often used to track you across many websites
They prevent the page from loading
mcqs.answer: They are often used to track you across many websites

mcqs.stem: Why might you turn off GPS / location on a social media app?
options: To stop posts and photos sharing where you are
mcqs.answer: To stop posts and photos sharing where you are

mcqs.stem: What feature of a website shows that it is secure for entering personal details?
options: Padlock symbol and 'https://' in the URL
mcqs.answer: Padlock symbol and 'https://' in the URL

mcqs.stem: Which of these suggests a shopping website might NOT be legitimate?
options: Lots of spelling mistakes and a strange domain
mcqs.answer: Lots of spelling mistakes and a strange domain

mcqs.stem: Why is it useful to check independent reviews before buying from a new online shop?
options: To see what real customers say about delivery, quality and refunds
Reviews change the price you pay
mcqs.answer: To see what real customers say about delivery, quality and refunds

mcqs.stem: Which is generally the most secure way to pay online?
options: Credit card or trusted payment service (e.g. PayPal)
Share your full bank details by email
mcqs.answer: Credit card or trusted payment service (e.g. PayPal)

mcqs.stem: What are 'marketing preferences' on an online account?
options: Settings to choose which messages and offers a company can send you
mcqs.answer: Settings to choose which messages and offers a company can send you

mcqs.stem: What is a cookie on a website?
options: A small file the site uses to remember information about you
mcqs.answer: A small file the site uses to remember information about you

mcqs.context: An online form will not submit and shows a red message next to the email field.
mcqs.stem: What is the MOST LIKELY cause?
options: The email is in an invalid format
mcqs.answer: The email is in an invalid format

mcqs.stem: What is a health risk of spending too much time online?
mcqs.answer: Eyestrain

mcqs.stem: Which is a good way to reduce eye strain when working on a screen?
options: Take regular breaks and look away
Turn the brightness up to maximum
Remove the light from the room
mcqs.answer: Take regular breaks and look away

mcqs.stem: Which is good practice for posture at a desk?
options: Top of the screen roughly at eye level, feet flat on the floor
Screen as low as possible, feet crossed
Hold the laptop on your lap with knees up
mcqs.answer: Top of the screen roughly at eye level, feet flat on the floor

mcqs.stem: How can heavy screen use late at night affect your health?
mcqs.answer: It can disturb sleep patterns

mcqs.stem: Which of these could you do as part of the 'right to be forgotten' in data protection law?
options: Ask for a copy of your personal data held by a bank or business
Check if your data is accurate
Ask for your data to be processed lawfully
Check that your personal data that is no longer necessary has been deleted
mcqs.answer: Check that your personal data that is no longer necessary has been deleted

mcqs.stem: Which UK law mainly protects how organisations use your personal data?
mcqs.answer: UK GDPR / Data Protection Act

mcqs.stem: Under data protection law, what does 'withdrawing consent' allow you to do?
options: Tell an organisation to stop using personal data you previously agreed to share
Take back money you paid online
Cancel any contract for any reason
mcqs.answer: Tell an organisation to stop using personal data you previously agreed to share

mcqs.stem: Which of these is a type of malware?
mcqs.answer: Trojan

mcqs.stem: What does ransomware do?
options: Encrypts your files and demands payment to unlock them
Saves your photos to the cloud
mcqs.answer: Encrypts your files and demands payment to unlock them

mcqs.stem: What should you do to reduce the risk of malware?
options: Use tags in social media posts
mcqs.answer: Install anti-virus software

mcqs.context: An email claims to be from your bank and asks you to click a link to 'verify' your account.
mcqs.stem: What is the safest action?
options: Click the link and enter your details
Open your banking app or type the bank's address yourself
mcqs.answer: Open your banking app or type the bank's address yourself

mcqs.stem: You need to email a colleague a document containing personal information. What is the best way to send it?
options: Send it in the body of the email
Include the information in a password protected attachment
mcqs.answer: Include the information in a password protected attachment

mcqs.stem: What is two-factor authentication (2FA)?
options: A second check (e.g. a code on your phone) when you log in
Two passwords on the same line
mcqs.answer: A second check (e.g. a code on your phone) when you log in

mcqs.stem: What is a benefit of using a password manager?
options: You can have a unique strong password for every account without remembering them all
mcqs.answer: You can have a unique strong password for every account without remembering them all

mcqs.stem: What is the main benefit of backing up files to the cloud?
options: The files can be recovered if your device is lost
It makes the device run faster
mcqs.answer: The files can be recovered if your device is lost

mcqs.stem: The '3-2-1 backup rule' suggests how many copies of important files?
options: 3 copies in total (2 on different media, 1 off-site)
mcqs.answer: 3 copies in total (2 on different media, 1 off-site)

mcqs.stem: Why is automatic cloud backup more reliable than remembering to back up by hand?
options: You can't forget to do it — it happens in the background
It removes the need for anti-virus
mcqs.answer: You can't forget to do it — it happens in the background
